evals.prompt: Generate the Kiro tasks.md for the avatar upload feature from its design.
evals.expected_output: An ordered checkbox task list with small tasks, sub-tasks, and per-task requirement references.
expectations: Uses a numbered checkbox list with sub-tasks
Each task cites the requirements/design it implements (e.g. _Requirements: 1.2_)
Tasks are small, incremental, and test-driven
Emits MIF frontmatter type: procedural and passes mif-validate --level 1

evals.prompt: Our task list is three giant bullets with no references. Make it Kiro-conformant.
evals.expected_output: Breaks tasks into small checkbox items with traceability and ordering.
expectations: Converts giant tasks into small, checkable sub-tasks

evals.prompt: Why does each Kiro task reference a requirement number?
evals.expected_output: Explains traceability: every task implements a requirement, and every requirement should have a task.
expectations: Explains traceability from tasks back to requirements/design
Notes a requirement with no task is unimplemented
Notes tasks.md is generated from design.md and drives implementation
